shell.demo_banner: Demo mode — read-only view with sample data. Data changes are disabled.
shell.footer_tagline: FTTH network management platform for Indonesian ISPs

shell.rx_empty: No RX history in this range yet. Wait for the next RX polling cycle.

login.welcome_subtitle: Sign in to access the NMS Dashboard.

forgot.title: Forgot Password
forgot.intro: Forgot your password? No problem. Just let us know your email address and we will email you a password reset link that will allow you to choose a new one.

confirm.title: Confirm Password
confirm.intro: This is a secure area of the application. Please confirm your password before continuing.

verify.title: Email Verification
verify.intro: Thanks for signing up! Before getting started, could you verify your email address by clicking on the link we just emailed to you? If you didn't receive the email, we will gladly send you another.
verify.sent: A new verification link has been sent to the email address you provided during registration.

dashboard.no_onu_data: No ONU data yet. Run polling.
dashboard.hero_subtitle: Monitor and manage OLT and ONU devices from one place.

modal.reset_body: The ONU reset endpoint is not available yet.
modal.upgrade_body: Bulk firmware upgrade is not available yet.
modal.log_body: The ONU log page is not available yet.
modal.not_implemented: This feature is not implemented in the backend yet.

smartolt.alarm_off_partner: Your webhook alarms are off — click to turn on

smartolt.confirm_save_msg: Save (write) the running config to OLT {name}'s memory? On OLTs with a large config (e.g. ZTE C300) this can take ~30 seconds.

portonus.search_placeholder: Search interface, serial, name, or type...
portonus.empty_sub: Run Refresh ONU to read the ONUs registered on this port.
portonus.nomatch_sub: Try changing the keyword or resetting the filter.
portonus.addmap_paste_hint: The pin is placed automatically at that link's coordinates.
portonus.addmap_click_hint: Open the ONU Map, then click a location to place the pin.
portonus.reboot_msg: Reboot {interface}? The ONU will restart in 30-60 seconds.
portonus.delete_msg: Delete {interface} from the OLT? The ONU registration will be permanently removed (no onu {onuId}).
portonus.coord_not_found: No coordinates found in the link.
portonus.gmaps_failed: Failed to process the Google Maps link.
portonus.copy_modal_title: Copy ONU config to another port
portonus.copy_modal_sub: {count} ONUs from Slot {slot} Port {port} will get a registration script generated on the destination port (same OLT).
portonus.ports_not_refreshed: The port list hasn't been refreshed — enter the destination slot/port manually.
portonus.execute_after: Execute to the OLT immediately after generating
portonus.execute_hint: (without this, the script is saved as generated for review first).
portonus.copy_warning: The onu-id on the destination port is allocated automatically (lowest free slot) — refresh the destination port first for accuracy. GPON SNs are unique: on real execution make sure the ONU has been physically moved / removed from the source port so the OLT does not reject it.
portonus.running_sub: Reading config & preparing {count} ONUs for the destination port. Runs in the background — you may close this; results are still saved in Registrations.

tr069.intro: Enables TR069 (ACS management) on all ONUs on port {slot}/{port} of {olt}. ONUs whose TR069 is already active & pointing to the target ACS are automatically skipped.
tr069.dryrun_hint: Run a Scan (Dry-run) first — it writes nothing to the OLT, only showing which ONUs will be activated and which are already active.
tr069.running_sub: Reading each ONU's running-config on port {slot}/{port} ({olt}). Runs in the background — you may close this; results are still saved.
tr069.no_cache: No ONUs cached for port {slot}/{port} — run Refresh ONU on this page first.
tr069.all_active: All ONUs already have TR069 active to the target ACS — nothing to execute.
tr069.will_write: {applied} ONUs will have TR069 config written directly to the OLT. This changes production ONUs.

unconfigured.title: Unconfigured ONUs
unconfigured.empty_discovery: No data yet. Run Refresh Discovery.
unconfigured.select_olt_hint: Select an OLT above to see unconfigured ONUs

gponports.title: GPON Port & ONU
gponports.empty_ports: No port data yet. Run Refresh SNMP.

configonu.readonly_notice: This OLT (e.g. ZTE C600) is view-only here — the real running-config is shown, but editing & saving are disabled because C600-style config writes (vport model) aren't supported yet.
configonu.readonly_editor_note: View-only mode: use the "Raw running-config" panel on the left to inspect the ONU configuration. The editor and Apply button are disabled for this OLT.
configonu.warning: Warning: Any change to VLAN, service-port, or WAN credentials will disconnect the customer for ±5-10 seconds (re-PPPoE auth). Make sure the SN & ONU ID are correct before Apply.
configonu.chassis_not_polled: Not polled yet — click Refresh Hardware
configonu.chassis_note: GPON ports are live from SNMP; uplink ports & per-board CPU/Mem bars fill in after Refresh Hardware. Hover a board for processor details; click a port for details (traffic & SFP).

registrations.title: Registration History
registrations.desc_failed: Last execution failed; can be retried.
registrations.desc_pending: Not sent to the OLT CLI yet.
registrations.confirm_exec_msg_new: Execute the provisioning script for {port} to the OLT?
registrations.confirm_exec_msg_retry: Re-run the provisioning script for {port} to the OLT?
registrations.confirm_del_msg: Delete the provisioning script for {customer} · {port}? This cannot be undone.

configbackups.title: Config Backup
configbackups.unsupported: Running-config backup is currently only available for ZTE OLTs (via CLI show running-config). This OLT is not supported.
configbackups.daily_hint: When enabled, this OLT's config is backed up daily (02:30) — only new versions are saved when changed.
configbackups.backup_now_hint: Capture the OLT's current running-config manually (takes a few seconds).
configbackups.empty: No backups yet. Click Backup to capture the first snapshot.
configbackups.no_diff: No differences between the two versions.
configbackups.diff_load_failed: Failed to load the versions to compare.

alarms.subtitle: Automatic evaluation results from the background poll.
alarms.empty_normal: Everything was normal on the last poll.

portdetail.no_data: No detail data for this port yet. Click Refresh from OLT to fetch status, traffic & SFP via CLI.
portdetail.sfp_empty: SFP data not available yet. Click Refresh from OLT.
portdetail.live_hint: Enable Live Traffic for a real-time chart (polls every 10 seconds via CLI).

registeronu.subtitle: {olt} · ONU provisioning + direct execution to the OLT
registeronu.preview_hint: The script that will be executed on the OLT
registeronu.preview_note: The preview updates automatically as the form changes. Real execution only runs when you press Execute to OLT.
registeronu.mode_hint: Simple: 1-service template. Advanced: configure tcont/gemport/service per row.
registeronu.identity_hint: Serial number, port position, and customer data
registeronu.adv_identity_hint: SN & port position — the customer name goes in the Name field below.
registeronu.gpon_hint: ONU type, TCONT, VLAN, and service name
registeronu.dhcp_note: DHCP mode — IP assigned automatically by the server, no extra fields.
registeronu.bridge_note: Bridge mode — the ONU becomes a pure L2 bridge (transparent VLAN, use the VLAN ID above, e.g. 100). No wan-ip/PPPoE/TR069 on the OLT; the customer's router does PPPoE. Suited to bridge-style OLTs (e.g. Bulumanis Lor). VLAN Profile is ignored in this mode.
registeronu.extra_hint: TR069 remote management and Remote ONT (optional)
registeronu.no_cli_note: This OLT driver does not support automatic CLI execution — the script can only be generated & saved to the audit log.
registeronu.confirm_exec_msg: Register & execute ONU {sn} to {olt} now? The script will run immediately via Telnet CLI.
registeronu.confirm_exec_msg_adv: Register & execute ONU {sn} to {olt} now? The granular script will run immediately via Telnet CLI.
registeronu.fill_form_comment: # Fill the form to see the script…

cdataform.identity_sub: Device name, C-Data family, and IP address
cdataform.family_hint: The family determines the driver. The Test button verifies it via sysObjectID (C-Data 17409/34592, HiOSO 25355).
cdataform.snmp_sub: C-Data only supports SNMP v1/v2c (port 161 by default)
cdataform.write_hint: Optional — C-Data ONU writes are often rejected; actions go via CLI instead.
cdataform.cli_sub: Required for GPON FlashV3.x inventory (show ont info all)
cdataform.poll_sub: Automatic scheduled scan (EPON via SNMP, GPON V3 via SNMP+CLI) — refreshes inventory & ONU RX
cdataform.poll_note: Note: GPON V3 uses telnet while scanning; too short an interval can overload the OLT.
cdataform.hioso_identity_sub: Device name & IP address for HiOSO / V-Sol EPON (25355)
cdataform.hioso_family_hint: The family is fixed to HiOSO 25355. The Test button verifies it via sysObjectID.
cdataform.hioso_snmp_sub: HiOSO only supports SNMP v1/v2c (port 161 by default)
cdataform.hioso_write_hint: Optional — HiOSO ONU write actions go via CLI, not SNMP.
cdataform.hioso_cli_sub: Telnet — required for ONU rename & reboot (interface epon 0/{port})
cdataform.hioso_poll_sub: Automatic scheduled scan via SNMP — refreshes inventory & ONU RX

cdatadetail.empty_ports: No port data yet. Click Scan ONUs to load.

cdataportonus.empty_hint: Click Refresh to scan this port.
cdataportonus.rename_label: Name / description (max 128, leave blank to clear)
cdataportonus.reboot_msg: Reboot ONU {onu}? The customer service drops for ~30–60 seconds.
cdataportonus.toggle_disable_msg: Disable ONU {onu}? The customer service will be down until re-enabled.
cdataportonus.delete_msg: Permanently delete ONU {onu} from the OLT? The ONU registration will be removed (ont delete) and must be re-registered to reactivate.
cdataportonus.delete_msg_hioso: Permanently delete ONU {onu} from the OLT? The ONU registration will be removed (no onu {onuId}) and must be re-registered to reactivate.

map.title: ONU Map
map.stats: {pins} pins · {online} online · customer ONUs across all OLTs
map.add_mode_active: Add mode active — click the map
map.placement_hint: Click a location on the map to place the selected ONU pin.
map.empty_after: then click a location on the map.
map.search_placeholder: Search ONU: interface, serial, customer name, or OLT...
map.customer_name_placeholder: Uses the ONU name when empty
map.delete_msg: Remove this ONU pin from the map? The ONU on the OLT is not affected.

panduan.title: User Guide
panduan.hero_desc: A complete guide to the app's features — from login, managing OLTs, ONU registration, monitoring & alarms, to settings. Use the table of contents to jump to the part you need.
panduan.footer_before: Need more technical detail? Developer docs live in
panduan.footer_after: . For direct help, contact your system admin.
panduan.pengantar_intro: An FTTH/GPON network management system for monitoring OLTs and provisioning ONUs from a single web dashboard — a SmartOLT/NetNumen alternative for ISPs.
panduan.pengantar_i0: ZTE (C300/C320), C-Data (EPON/GPON), and HiOSO/V-Sol (EPON) managed side by side.
panduan.pengantar_i1: scheduled SNMP polling collects PON port status, ONU online/offline, and RX levels; alarms are sent automatically to Telegram & the mobile app.
panduan.pengantar_i2: register new ONUs, configure WAN (PPPoE/DHCP/static), VLAN, TR069, plus reboot/rename/delete — straight from the web.
panduan.pengantar_tip: Press Ctrl/⌘ + K anywhere for global search (find an OLT or ONU by name/serial/interface).
panduan.peran_intro: Log in with the email & password provided by your admin. The menus and buttons you see depend on your account's role.
panduan.peran_i0: full access: manage OLTs, users, system settings, audit logs, and every ONU action.
panduan.peran_i1: day-to-day operations: view OLT/ONU, monitoring, ONU registration & actions. Cannot manage users/settings.
panduan.peran_i2: sees only the OLTs assigned to them (private OLTs are hidden from other admins/operators), plus their own Telegram bot.
panduan.peran_i3: showcase account: free to explore, but write actions (registration, reboot, delete, etc.) are blocked.
panduan.navigasi_intro: The left sidebar is the main menu. The top bar holds search, the notification bell, and the account menu.
panduan.navigasi_i0: click the arrow icon to collapse/expand; on mobile, tap the menu icon to open it.
panduan.navigasi_i1: type an OLT name/IP or an ONU serial/name/interface to jump straight to it.
panduan.navigasi_i2: shows the latest alarms; the red number = active alarm count.
panduan.navigasi_i3: edit your profile/password and log out.
panduan.dashboard_intro: The first page after login: an overall summary of network health.
panduan.dashboard_i0: Stat cards: number of OLTs, ONUs online/offline, PON ports, and active alarms.
panduan.dashboard_i1: Highlights of recent alarms and problematic OLTs for quick follow-up.
panduan.dashboard_i2: Numbers come from the latest polling results — they reflect the last few minutes, not second-by-second real time.
panduan.olt_intro: The SmartOLT menu lists OLTs in per-vendor tabs (ZTE, C-Data, HiOSO). Add one via the Add OLT button.
panduan.olt_i0: name, IP, SNMP port & communities (read/write), and CLI/telnet credentials if you plan to provision.
panduan.olt_i1: click to verify the OLT is reachable — it shows system info, PON ports, and the ONU count.
panduan.olt_i2: so the OLT joins scheduled monitoring (ONU status & RX are refreshed periodically by the poller engine).
panduan.olt_i3: the On/Off button in the OLT list controls whether this OLT's alarm notifications are sent (evaluation still runs & is recorded).
panduan.olt_tip: Secret fields (write community, CLI password) can be left blank while editing to keep the old values — no need to retype them.
panduan.port-onu_intro: From an OLT's detail page, open a PON port to see the ONUs beneath it with their status and RX levels.
panduan.port-onu_i0: online/offline, last down cause (LOS, dying gasp), and interface.
panduan.port-onu_i1: signal-quality indicator; values outside the healthy range are flagged as warnings.
panduan.port-onu_i2: the refresh button performs a live SNMP read for that port/OLT (rate-limited to avoid stressing the OLT).
panduan.unconfigured_intro: The Unconfigured menu collects ONUs detected by the OLT but not yet registered — new-customer candidates.
panduan.unconfigured_i0: Find new ONUs by serial number on their PON port.
panduan.unconfigured_i1: Click Register to open the provisioning form with the serial pre-filled.
panduan.provisioning_intro: Registering a new ONU on a ZTE OLT: pick profiles, define the WAN service, then run the generated CLI script.
panduan.provisioning_i0: ONU type, T-CONT, and VLAN/IP profiles (synced per-OLT from the OLT).
panduan.provisioning_i1: PPPoE (user/pass), DHCP, or static IP; add VLAN and TR069 as needed.
panduan.provisioning_i2: the system builds the registration CLI script — review it before executing.
panduan.provisioning_i3: the script runs on the OLT via telnet; the result is recorded as a registration audit.
panduan.provisioning_tip: Every provisioning writes an audit row (script generated first, executed later) so its trail can be traced.
panduan.aksi-onu_intro: Maintenance actions are available from the per-port ONU page. Availability depends on the OLT vendor's capabilities.
panduan.aksi-onu_i1: change the ONU description/name (e.g. the customer name).
panduan.aksi-onu_i2: remove the ONU registration from the port (where the vendor supports it).
panduan.aksi-onu_i3: read the source ONU's running-config, then rebuild the registration on the target port (batch, with a progress modal).
panduan.aksi-onu_i4: enable TR069/ACS for every ONU on one PON port at once (ZTE OLTs).
panduan.monitoring_intro: The ONU Monitoring menu merges ONUs from every OLT into one view for network-wide monitoring.
panduan.monitoring_i0: See the online/offline spread of ONUs across OLTs without opening them one by one.
panduan.monitoring_i1: Refresh runs a full SNMP read per OLT and updates the per-port cache.
panduan.peta_intro: The ONU Map menu shows customer-ONU location pins on a map (Leaflet) for all OLTs.
panduan.peta_i0: click the map then pick OLT → port → ONU, or use the Add Map button on the per-port ONU page.
panduan.peta_i1: paste a location link and the coordinates are picked up automatically.
panduan.peta_i2: the pin detail card can rename or reboot the ONU straight from the map.
panduan.alarm_intro: The Alarms menu is the alarm hub. The system raises alarms only on healthy-to-fault transitions (e.g. ONU online → offline, port up → down, RX out of range).
panduan.alarm_i0: OLT unreachable, PON port down, LOS, dying gasp, ONU offline, and high RX attenuation.
panduan.alarm_i1: in Settings → Alarm tab, the admin chooses whether notifications go out immediately (realtime) or wait until the fault is confirmed across 2 checks (anti-flap, default).
panduan.alarm_i2: per-OLT (admin/operator) and per-partner alarm switches control who receives notifications — alarm evaluation keeps running.
panduan.alarm_i3: notifications are forwarded to the Telegram bot and mobile app push (FCM) per your settings.
panduan.alarm_tip: The default "2-poll confirmation" mode holds an alarm until the fault is seen in two consecutive polls (~10 minutes) so brief flaps don't fire false notifications. Need alerts ASAP? Switch to Realtime.
panduan.telnet_intro: Open a CLI session to the OLT right from the browser (xterm.js terminal window) — no separate telnet app.
panduan.telnet_i0: The window can be dragged, minimized, and maximized.
panduan.telnet_i1: Handy for manual/diagnostic CLI commands on ZTE OLTs.
panduan.report_intro: The Report menu builds filterable, exportable reports.
panduan.report_i1: per OLT, per PON port, time range, status, and RX attenuation status.
panduan.report_i2: download as CSV or PDF via the buttons at the top right.
panduan.pengaturan_intro: The Settings menu (admin only) holds several system configuration tabs.
panduan.pengaturan_i1: default ACS endpoint for the bulk TR069 feature.
panduan.pengaturan_i2: choose notification behavior: Realtime or 2-poll confirmation (anti-flap).
panduan.pengaturan_i3: bot token, chat id, minimum severity, triggers (raise/clear), alarm-type filter, and the bot command webhook.
panduan.pengaturan_i4: FCM push settings for the Android app + send a manual notification.
panduan.pengaturan_i5: issue/revoke API tokens (for integrations & the mobile app).
panduan.users_intro: Admins manage accounts and trace system activity.
panduan.users_i1: a trail of important changes (who changed what, and when).
panduan.partner_intro: Partner accounts only see the OLTs assigned to them and manage their own Telegram bot.
panduan.partner_i0: a partner's OLTs are hidden from other admins/operators.
panduan.partner_i1: partners set up their own bot & notification recipients, independent of the global bot.
panduan.mobile_intro: An Android app is available for monitoring on mobile devices.
panduan.mobile_i0: Download the APK from the link in Settings (when available on the server).
panduan.mobile_i1: Log in with the same account; supports dashboard, search, OLT→port→ONU, unconfigured+basic registration, reboot/rename, alarms, and push notifications.
panduan.troubleshooting_i0: check the SNMP IP/port/community and make sure the OLT allows SNMP access from the server.
panduan.troubleshooting_i1: that's the 2-poll confirmation mode (default). For faster alerts, enable Realtime in Settings → Alarm.
panduan.troubleshooting_i2: check that the OLT alarm switch is On and Telegram/Mobile settings are enabled with matching severity/types.
panduan.troubleshooting_i3: your role may lack permission, the account may be in demo mode, or the OLT vendor doesn't support that action.

users.title: User Management
users.empty_sub: Add the first user to get started.
users.modal_create_sub: Fill in the details to create a new account.
users.assign_hint_operator: The operator is limited to the checked OLTs. Leave empty to grant access to all OLTs.
users.assign_hint_partner: The partner can only view & edit the OLTs checked here.
users.no_olt: No OLTs registered yet. Add an OLT first.
users.password_new: New Password (leave blank to keep the current one)
users.delete_msg: Delete user "{name}" ({email})? This action is permanent.

auditlogs.title: Audit Logs
auditlogs.list_sub: A record of user actions & data changes in the system.
auditlogs.empty_filtered: No audit logs match the filter.

profile.title: Profile
profile.info_sub: Update your account's profile information and email address.
profile.name: Name
profile.resend: Click here to re-send the verification email.
profile.verification_sent: A new verification link has been sent to your email address.
profile.password_sub: Ensure your account is using a long, random password to stay secure.
profile.delete_sub: Once your account is deleted, all of its resources and data will be permanently deleted. Before deleting your account, please download any data or information that you wish to retain.
profile.delete_confirm_title: Are you sure you want to delete your account?
profile.delete_confirm_sub: Once your account is deleted, all of its resources and data will be permanently deleted. Please enter your password to confirm you would like to permanently delete your account.

telegrambot.scope_before: This bot only receives alarms & serves commands for the
telegrambot.scope_after: assigned to your account by the admin.
telegrambot.section_sub_partner: Send your OLT/ONU alarms to your own Telegram bot or group.
telegrambot.enable_hint: When off, alarms are still stored but not sent to Telegram.
telegrambot.token_saved_placeholder: •••••••• (token saved — leave blank to keep it)
telegrambot.token_hint: Get a token from @BotFather on Telegram via the /newbot command.
telegrambot.chat_id_placeholder: e.g. 123456789 or -1001234567890. Separate multiple IDs with commas.
telegrambot.chat_id_hint: For personal chats: message the bot then check @userinfobot. For groups: add the bot to the group; group IDs start with a minus sign.
telegrambot.min_severity_hint: Only alarms at this severity or higher are sent.
telegrambot.on_raise: Send when a new alarm is raised
telegrambot.on_raise_hint: Notify when a new alarm triggers during a polling cycle.
telegrambot.on_clear_hint: Notify when an active alarm returns to normal.
telegrambot.types_hint: Only the checked alarm types are sent to Telegram.
telegrambot.types_none: Nothing checked — all alarm notifications are off.
telegrambot.webhook_sub_partner: The bot replies to commands from registered chat IDs (read-only, your OLTs only).
telegrambot.commands_hint: When enabled & the webhook is registered, the bot answers /status, /olt, /alarm, /onu.
telegrambot.webhook_need_token: Save the bot token first before registering the webhook.
telegrambot.need_token_chat: Save the token & chat ID first to send a test.
telegrambot.section_sub_admin: Send the latest OLT/ONU alarms to a Telegram bot or group.
telegrambot.webhook_sub_admin: The bot replies to commands from registered chat IDs (read-only).
telegrambot.commands_hint_admin: When enabled & the webhook is registered, the bot answers /status, /olt, /alarm, /onu, /prov.
telegrambot.types_hint_admin: Only the checked alarm types are sent to Telegram. E.g. check only LOS, Dying Gasp, High RX attenuation, and GPON port down.

settings.title: Settings
settings.identity_sub: The name, version, and logo shown across the app.
settings.logo_hint: PNG, JPG, WEBP, or SVG. Max 1 MB. Leave empty to use the default logo.
settings.app_version_hint: Shown on the system panel in the sidebar.
settings.android_sub: Download & install the KusumaVision NMS mobile app.
settings.apk_missing_hint: Build it on the server first with bash bin/build-apk.sh; the result is copied automatically to public/downloads/.
settings.acs_sub: Used by the "Bulk TR069" feature on the per-port ONU page (ZTE OLTs).
settings.acs_url_hint: Full ACS server address including the port, e.g. http://acs.example.net:7547.
settings.acs_pw_hint: Leave blank to keep the old password.
settings.acs_note: The bulk TR069 scan marks an ONU as already active only when its TR069 is unlocked and its URL + username exactly match the values above. ONUs that are inactive or point to a different ACS are rewritten with this URL, username, and password.
settings.alarm_sub: When alarm notifications (Telegram & mobile) are sent after a fault is detected.
settings.alarm_toggle_hint: On: the fault must still be present at the next check (2× polls, ~10 minutes) before a notification is sent — damping false alarms from brief flaps. Off: realtime, notifications go out as soon as the fault is first detected.
settings.alarm_note: This setting applies globally to every OLT and every channel (Telegram + mobile), and takes effect on the next poll without a restart. Alarms are always recorded in the history; this only controls the notification timing. Realtime is faster but may notify about momentary faults that recover immediately.
settings.fcm_sub: Choose which alarms are pushed to the Android app via Firebase (FCM).
settings.fcm_creds: Firebase credentials are not installed on the server yet (service-account JSON + FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS). Pushes won't be sent until that is set up.
settings.fcm_enable_hint: When off, alarms are still stored but not sent to the mobile app.
settings.fcm_on_raise_hint: Push when a new alarm triggers.
settings.fcm_on_clear_hint: Push when an alarm returns to normal.
settings.fcm_types_hint: Only the checked alarm types are sent.
settings.fcm_types_none: Nothing checked — all alarm pushes are off.
settings.fcm_manual_sub: Broadcast a message to every registered mobile app ({n} devices).
settings.fcm_body_placeholder: Write the message that will appear in the phone notification…
settings.fcm_no_devices: No devices registered yet (users must log in on the app first).
settings.api_disabled_note: All /api endpoints are turned off for security (zero attack surface) since no app uses them yet. Tokens can still be prepared, but only work after the API is enabled on the server: set $apiEnabled = true in routes/api.php then reload PHP-FPM.
settings.api_access_sub: Tokens for calling NMS data from another web app or Android.
settings.api_base_hint: Token-protected endpoints, e.g. {url}/onus. Send the header Authorization: Bearer <token>.
settings.api_public_hint: Aggregate numbers without customer data — safe for widgets on other sites. Full documentation: docs/API.md.
settings.api_new_token_title: New token created — copy it now
settings.api_new_token_note: For security, the token is only shown once. Store it somewhere safe (e.g. the other app's server .env).
settings.api_personal_sub: Each token grants access as your account. Revoke it any time.
settings.token_name_placeholder: e.g. Billing Web, Technician Android App
settings.api_enable_first: Enable the API on the server first before creating tokens.
settings.tokens_empty: No tokens yet. Create one above to start using the API from another app.
settings.token_warning: Tokens are secrets, like passwords. Don't put them in code visible to the public/browser. For other websites, store them server-side (e.g. .env) and call the API from the server.
settings.revoke_confirm: Revoke this token? Apps using it will lose access immediately.
settings.devices_sub: Every phone logged into the mobile app (all users). Revoke a token to cut off a lost/unused phone.
settings.devices_empty: No devices have logged into the app yet.
settings.devices_revoke_confirm: Revoke this device token? The app on that phone loses access immediately and must log in again.
settings.devices_fcm_sub: Devices receiving push notifications. Delete to stop push to a specific phone (separate from login tokens).
settings.devices_fcm_delete_confirm: Delete this device push registration? That phone stops receiving notifications until it logs in again.

welcome.hero_desc: Monitor, provision, and manage ZTE C300/C320/C600 OLTs — now multi-vendor with C-Data & HiOSO — from one place, complete with an ONU location map and an Android app. Built for Indonesian ISP operations that demand speed and accuracy.
welcome.hw_desc: Mature SNMP & CLI drivers for ZTE C300, C320, and C600 — now extended to C-Data EPON (17409) & GPON (34592) plus HiOSO / V-Sol EPON (25355) OLTs. Battle-tested on Indonesian ISPs' production OLTs.
welcome.features_title: Everything You Need in One Platform
welcome.features_sub: Purpose-built for Indonesian ISP FTTH/GPON operations — from monitoring to remote management.
welcome.f_multivendor_body: Manage ZTE, C-Data (EPON & GPON), and HiOSO / V-Sol EPON OLTs side by side in one dashboard — cross-OLT ONU monitoring, plus rename, reboot & delete ONUs right from the dashboard.
welcome.f_map_body: Map your customer/ONU spread across OLTs on an interactive map — add pins from a map click or a Google Maps link, with ONU reboot & rename right from the customer's location.
welcome.f_android_body: An Android mobile app to watch OLTs & ONUs, register, reboot/rename, and receive real-time alarm push notifications via Firebase — the NOC stays connected anywhere.
welcome.f_inventory_body: Manage every OLT device, card, and GPON port in one centralized inventory with capability detection.
welcome.f_monitoring_body: Watch every ONU across OLTs & ports on one page — online/LOS/dying-gasp status, RX optical power, with quick filters per OLT, port, and status.
welcome.f_provisioning_body: Generate ZTE provisioning CLI scripts (register, T-CONT, VLAN, PPPoE/DHCP/Static/Bridge, TR-069) and execute them via Telnet — plus per-port bulk TR-069 & ONU config copy between ports.
welcome.f_polling_body: Scheduled polling for system info, port status, and the ONU table — all stored as per-OLT snapshots.
welcome.f_alarm_body: Signature-based alarm correlation with Critical/Major/Minor/Warning severities and auto-clear tracking.
welcome.f_remote_body: Reboot, enable/disable, and control ONUs remotely from the dashboard — no SSH into the OLT.
welcome.f_telnet_body: Access the OLT CLI right from the browser via an xterm.js terminal — draggable window, minimize/maximize, auto-login, no separate telnet app.
welcome.f_search_body: Find an OLT or ONU instantly by serial number, customer name, or interface — and jump straight to its port.
welcome.f_reports_body: Network statistics reports — port utilization, RX optical power (warning/critical), and OLT status — downloadable per OLT or time range.
welcome.f_notif_body: Important alarms go to Telegram groups/chats & Android push notifications, plus an interactive bot (button menus): check OLT & ONU status, find customers, even refresh an OLT from anywhere.
welcome.f_audit_body: An indelible audit trail for every configuration change, login, and telnet access — complete with actor, time, and old→new diff.
welcome.f_rbac_body: Separate access for admins, NOC operators, and partners — partners only see & manage their own private OLTs; sensitive features like audit logs & system settings stay admin-only.
welcome.how_title: From Device to Operations in 4 Steps
welcome.how_sub: The same workflow the NOC team uses every day — connected, monitored, and provisioned without hopping between tools.
welcome.step1_body: Register ZTE OLTs (IP, SNMP community, CLI credentials) into a centralized inventory with automatic capability detection.
welcome.step2_body: A Go engine polls system info, GPON port status, and the ONU table on schedule — stored as per-OLT snapshots.
welcome.step3_body: Generate ZTE CLI scripts (register, T-CONT, VLAN, PPPoE/DHCP/Static, TR-069) then execute them directly via Telnet from the browser.
welcome.step4_body: Watch RX power & ONU status, receive important alarms via Telegram, then download utilization & optical reports per time range.
welcome.gallery_sub: From the dashboard to ONU provisioning — a clean interface designed for NOC operational speed.
welcome.shot_portonus_desc: ONU list of one PON port
welcome.modules_sub: Every module is designed to be lean, with a workflow that feels natural to the NOC team.
welcome.m_dashboard_sub: At-a-glance view of the whole FTTH network
welcome.m_map_sub: Customer spread on an interactive map
welcome.m_telnet_sub: OLT CLI right from the browser
welcome.m_android_sub: Monitor & manage from your phone + FCM push
welcome.cta_title: Ready to Manage Your FTTH Network More Efficiently?
welcome.cta_sub: Start monitoring OLTs & ONUs with a centralized & consistent dashboard.
welcome.footer_desc: An FTTH network management platform for Indonesian ISPs. Developed with a focus on fast & consistent NOC operations.
